Robinia pseudoacacia, commonly known as Black Locust, is a flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae). The health benefits of Robinia pseudoacacia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Robinia pseudoacacia include robinin, robinidine, and robinoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Robinia pseudoacacia include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
